source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that the --client-cert-auth argument is set to true

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that the --client-cert-auth argument is set to true
input_row.description: Enable	client	authentication	on	etcd	service.
input_row.rationale: etcd	is	a	highly-available	key	value	store	used	by	Kubernetes	deployments	for	persistent storage	of	all	of	its	REST	API	objects.	These	objects	are	sensitive	in	nature	and	should	not be	available	to	unauthenticated	clients.	You	should	enable	the	client	authentication	via valid	certificates	to	secure	the	access	to	the	etcd	service. Impact: All	clients	attempting	to	access	the	etcd	server	will	require	a	valid	client	certificate.
input_row.audit: Run	the	following	command	on	the	etcd	server	node: ps -ef | grep etcd Verify	that	the	--client-cert-auth	argument	is	set	to	true.
input_row.remediation: Edit	the	etcd	pod	specification	file	/etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml	on	the	master node	and	set	the	below	parameter. --client-cert-auth="true" Default	Value: By	default,	the	etcd	service	can	be	queried	by	unauthenticated	clients. References: 1. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/security.html 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/etcd/ 142	|	P a g e 3. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/configuration.html#client-cert-auth Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 143	|	P a g e
input_row.impact: All	clients	attempting	to	access	the	etcd	server	will	require	a	valid	client	certificate.
input_row.references: 1. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/security.html 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/etcd/ 142	|	P a g e 3. https://coreos.com/etcd/docs/latest/op-guide/configuration.html#client-cert-auth Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 143	|	P a g e
input_row.justification: The audit requires verification of the etcd process arguments, specifically `--client-cert-auth`, which are set at the process or pod specification level on the master node. Alibaba Cloud APIs, CLI, or ACK console do not expose etcd process arguments or manifest file contents for managed clusters. While Kubernetes-native tools like `kubectl` can sometimes inspect pod specs, in ACK the etcd component is managed by the control plane and is not accessible via standard Kubernetes API or Alibaba Cloud APIs. Therefore, SSH access and direct inspection are required.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. SSH into the master node of your Kubernetes cluster where the etcd service is running. 2. Run the following command to list the running etcd process and its arguments: ```bash ps -ef | grep etcd ``` 3. Examine the output to locate the `--client-cert-auth` argument. 4. Confirm that `--client-cert-auth` is explicitly set to `true`. 5. If the argument is missing or set to `false`, edit the etcd pod specification file (usually `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml`) to include or update `--client-cert-auth="true"`. 6. Save the file and allow the etcd pod to restart with the new configuration. 7. Re-run the `ps -ef | grep etcd` command to confirm the change.
input_row.default_value: By default, the etcd service can be queried by unauthenticated clients.
input_row.additional_information: This control cannot be audited and modified in ACK managed clusters.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit process requires SSH access to the master node to check the running etcd process for the `--client-cert-auth` argument. This information is not available through Alibaba Cloud APIs or CLI tools for ACK managed clusters as the etcd service is controlled by the ACK control plane and its configurations are not exposed for direct visibility.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. SSH into the master node of your Kubernetes cluster where the etcd service is running.
2. Run the following command to list the running etcd process and its arguments:
   ```bash
   ps -ef | grep etcd
   ```
3. Examine the output to locate the `--client-cert-auth` argument.
4. Confirm that `--client-cert-auth` is explicitly set to `true`.
5. If the argument is missing or set to `false`, edit the etcd pod specification file (usually `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml`) to include or update `--client-cert-auth="true"`.
6. Save the file and allow the etcd pod to restart with the new configuration.
7. Re-run the `ps -ef | grep etcd` command to confirm the change, ensuring that `--client-cert-auth` is set to `true`.
